evidence.scientific_clarity: Grouped bar chart clearly shows 4 methods × 5 benchmarks with error bars; winner (OursModel ★) is visually distinct via bold edges and star label; y-axis starts near data minimum per brief hint.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype=none are set in rcParams, but pdffonts reveals the embedded font is 'DejaVuSans' — the default matplotlib font rather than a preferred publication font (e.g. Helvetica/Arial/Times); text sizes (6.5–8 pt) are acceptable for single-column.
evidence.palette: Uses a 4-color Seaborn-derived palette (#4C72B0, #DD8452, #55A868, #C44E52) that is colorblind-safe for the most common deficiencies; hatches added for B&W readers; no green/red directional misuse.
evidence.layout_density: 3.5×2.6 in figure fits a Nature single-column width; tight_layout used; no overlapping labels observed; y-range trimmed above data minimum; bars are well-separated with appropriate width.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content (4 methods, 5 benchmarks, std error bars, winner); legend placed upper-left with 2 columns; star marker in legend matches bar label; no obvious Reviewer 2 complaint visible.
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf is a real PDF (27 KB, non-trivial); pdf.fonttype=42 is set so text is TrueType/editable in theory, but pdffonts shows font is subsetted CID TrueType — selectable but subset-named (BMQQDV+DejaVuSans); vector paths intact.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (27 KB), figure.png (66 KB), figure.svg (95 KB), caption.tex (414 B), gen_figure.py (2.5 KB) all exist and are non-trivial; brief required .pdf/.png/caption.tex/gen_figure.py — all present.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names chart type, variables (4 methods, 5 benchmarks, accuracy %), error bar definition (std over 5 seeds), and includes a specific numerical finding ('4--13 percentage points') with the winner identified.

overall_strengths: Colorblind-safe palette with hatches for B&W readers, tight layout sized for single-column publication, and clear winner highlighting satisfy all brief requirements.
Caption is publication-ready: specifies chart type, data source, error bar definition, and a concrete quantitative finding (4–13 pp margins).
overall_weaknesses: Font is DejaVuSans (matplotlib default) rather than a preferred publication typeface (Helvetica, Arial, or a serif); a journal style guide would likely flag this.

PDF font is subset-named (BMQQDV+DejaVuSans) which means text is technically selectable but the subset prefix makes it non-standard; switching rcParams font.family to 'Helvetica' or 'Arial' would resolve both issues.
